title: [VPlan] Use unsigned integers for lane start indices
author: boomanaiden154
description:
a83c89495ba6fe0134dcaa02372c320cc7ff0dbf caused assertion failures here as if we have a single bit induction variable and two lanes (0 and 1), then the second lane index (1) will be out of bounds of what a signed 1-bit integer can hold. Lane indices are always >0 according to VPlanHelpers.h:125, and the lane representation in this code is also unsigned.

The test case come from tensorflow/XLA.

tests.test_body:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-i128:128-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-grtev4-linux-gnu"

define void @copy_bitcast_fusion(ptr noalias %foo, ptr noalias %bar) {
header:
  br label %body

body:                                             ; preds = %body, %header
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %header ], [ %iv.next, %body ]
  %iv.trunc = trunc i64 %iv to i1
  %iv.trunc2 = select i1 %iv.trunc, i64 1, i64 0
  %load.addr = getelementptr float, ptr %foo, i64 %iv.trunc2
  %l1 = load float, ptr %load.addr, align 4
  %store.addr = getelementptr float, ptr %bar, i64 %iv
  store float %l1, ptr %store.addr, align 4
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 1
  %exitcond.not = icmp eq i64 %iv, 1
  br i1 %exitcond.not, label %exit, label %body

exit:                                             ; preds = %body
  ret void
}

comments.author: boomanaiden154
comments.body: (sorry for the force push. I was amending, forgetting I wasn't using `spr`)

comments.author: boomanaiden154
comments.body: Thank you very much for the fast review! Looks you already got the last issue we were seeing (1c07ba612d03b05b2ff72f36bd5da6b732f931e4).
